title: cockerellia cockerellii
intro: Cockerellia cockerellii, also known as Rock rose or Cockscomb, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cockerellia cockerellii include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation, and its potential to aid in the healing process of minor cuts and scrapes. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various skin issues.
